vulnerability.title: Enforce usage of pam_wheel for su authentication
vulnerability.description: To ensure that only users who are members of the wheel group can run commands with altered privileges through the su command, make sure that the following line exists in the file /etc/pam.d/su: auth required pam_wheel.so use_uid
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q pam; then # uncomment the option if commented sed '/^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*auth[[:space:]]\+required[[:space:]]\+pam_wheel\.so[[:space:]]\+use_uid$/s/^[[:space:]]*#//' -i /etc/pam.d/su else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: This PAM configuration change enforces wheel group membership for su command, aligning with STIG hardening standards. It is a low-risk modification with minimal chance of causing system breakage or lockout when properly implemented.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; access control modification

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The finding is about enforcing pam_wheel for su authentication. According to the remediation description, I need to ensure that the line "auth required pam_wheel.so use_uid" exists in /etc/pam.d/su. If it's commented out, I need to uncomment it.

Let me first read the file to see its current state, then apply the necessary changes.

My plan:
1. Read /etc/pam.d/su to see if the line exists and whether it's commented.
2. If the line exists but is commented, uncomment it using sed.
3. If the line doesn't exist, add it (but the remediation script seems to only handle uncommenting, so I'll follow that approach).

I'll start by reading the file.
reasoning_messages.content: I'll remediate the OpenSCAP finding by ensuring the pam_wheel configuration is properly set in /etc/pam.d/su. Let me first inspect the current configuration.
